heading: Considerations
body:
VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) peering connections Atlas have the following limitations:

- Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) supports two network modes: routes-based and VPC-native. While VPC-native GKE clusters
  can connect to Atlas clusters, route-based GKE clusters can't connect to Atlas clusters via peering because Atlas
  doesn't accept custom routes when VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) peering connections are created. Consider using Public IP allow lists for
  route-based GKE clusters.

- Google App Engine (Standard), Cloud Functions, and Cloud Run can't connect to Atlas clusters over VPC (Virtual Private Cloud)
  peering connections. To connect over VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) peering, these services require a serverless VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) Access connector.

- Clients can't connect to Atlas clusters with Google Cloud VPN (Virtual Private Network) or Interconnect because
  Atlas doesn't accept custom routes when VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) peering connections are created. Consider creating [private endpoint connections](private-endpoint).
